title: Document-Model Prim Spec Production Contract
summary: Format-neutral rules a prim spec must satisfy after parsing, regardless of whether the source layer was USDA text or USDC binary. Both contracts/usda-productions/prim-specs.contract.json and contracts/usdc-productions/prim-spec-mapping.contract.json cite this contract via derives_from for the document-model invariants; each format then adds storage_mapping describing how its on-disk encoding produces these fields.

specifier.required: always emitted on every prim spec; storage is native enum/domain value, not a durable string payload

typeName.required: only when authored; absent on prim specs that author no concrete typeName (including USDA-authored variant prim specs)

kind.required: only when authored; stored as authored metadata and not schema-interpreted by the parser

active.required: only when authored; active=false must not remove the spec at the layer-storage level

primChildren.required: every prim spec carries this field; the value is the ordered list of child prim spec names. Maintained by usd-document-model when child prims are added.

propertyChildren.required: every prim spec carries this field; the value is the ordered list of child property names (attributes and relationships). Maintained by usd-document-model when properties are added.

variantSetChildren.required: every prim spec carries this field; the value is the ordered list of authored variantSet spec names. Distinct from variantSetNames (which is authored composition metadata).

primOrder.required: only when authored via a child reordering; storage holds the last authored ordering when the source format permits multiple authored orderings

propertyOrder.required: only when authored via a property reordering

references.value_shape: each Reference item carries optional asset reference, optional target prim path, offset (default 0.0), scale (default 1.0). Authored layer data; composition is not evaluated by the parser. Native Reference records, not bare strings or JSON dictionaries, are required in durable storage.

payload.value_shape: each Payload item carries optional asset reference, optional target prim path, offset (default 0.0), scale (default 1.0). Native Payload records.

variantSetNames.required: only when authored. Distinct from variantSetChildren which lists spec storage.

variantSelection.required: only when authored; dictionary keys are variantSet names, values are selected variant names or empty strings. Authored layer data; the parser does not select or populate variants.

excluded_fields.policy: Strict AOUSD §7.6 conformance. Fields the spec marks Deprecated are NOT in the live field set: they are recognized as reserved spec tokens and rejected (ForbiddenFieldOnSpecKind), never admitted as live fields and never reinterpreted as generic customData/extension fields. noLoadHint is removed outright because AOUSD lists it only as a deprecated relationship field (§7.6.5.2), never on prims — its prim placement was OpenUSD-only.

removed_no_prim_spec_basis.note: Has no AOUSD prim-spec basis (live or deprecated); was OpenUSD-only.

listop_subfield_rules.single_item_is_explicit: When a source format authors a single item where a listOp is allowed without an explicit listOp prefix, the result is an explicit listOp containing that one item. This applies to references, payload, inheritPaths, specializes, variantSetNames, and connectionPaths.
listop_subfield_rules.none_is_empty_explicit: A None authored value where a listOp is allowed produces an empty listOp on the named subfield.
listop_subfield_rules.subfield_last_wins: Within a single source layer, repeated authored subfields of the same listOp use last-one-wins per subfield. Cross-layer composition is out of scope.

child_list_invariants.primChildren: the ordered list of names of every child prim spec materialized under this prim. Adding a child appends to this list; child-list and child-spec storage must agree.
child_list_invariants.propertyChildren: the ordered list of every attribute and relationship spec name materialized under this prim. Adding a property appends to this list.
child_list_invariants.variantSetChildren: the ordered list of every variantSet spec name materialized under this prim. Distinct from authored variantSetNames listOp metadata.
child_list_invariants.agreement_rule: for every spec name in a child-list field, a corresponding child spec must exist in document-model storage, and vice versa; mismatch is a contract violation.

laws: every prim spec must carry primChildren, propertyChildren, and variantSetChildren as the format-neutral required field set
primChildren, propertyChildren, and variantSetChildren are token[] fields maintained by usd-document-model in agreement with stored child specs
prim specifier is a native EnumSpecifier value (def/over/class), never a durable string or JSON payload
composition arc fields (references, payload, inheritPaths, specializes, variantSetNames) are authored layer data; their composition semantics are not evaluated by the parser
a single arc item authored without an explicit listOp prefix is stored as an explicit listOp containing that one item
None authored where a listOp is allowed produces an empty listOp value on the named subfield
reference and payload items must be stored as native arc records (asset, prim path, offset, scale), not as JSON dictionaries, bare asset strings, or bare path strings
variantSelection storage uses the native VariantSelectionMap value, not a string-keyed JSON object
active=false is preserved as field storage; the parser does not filter inactive prims from the layer
golden_groups: all prim specifiers (def, over, class) with and without typeName
nested prim child storage and primChildren maintenance
property child storage and propertyChildren maintenance
variantSet child storage and variantSetChildren maintenance
references and payload with asset, prim path, offset, and scale
inheritPaths and specializes single-item and listOp subfield forms
variantSetNames authored as listOp; variantSelection as VariantSelectionMap
kind, active, hidden, instanceable authored fields
